assumptions: The issue concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements for foreign exchange risk arising from an intragroup dividend.
The intragroup dividend may be considered either as an existing intragroup balance or as an intragroup forecast transaction, depending on the fact pattern.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for an intragroup monetary item
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 explicitly allows the foreign currency risk of an intragroup monetary item to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when the related exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation.

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for a highly probable intragroup forecast transaction
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 explicitly allows the foreign currency risk of a highly probable forecast intragroup transaction to qualify as a hedged item in consolidated financial statements when that risk will affect consolidated profit or loss.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the IFRS model for hedging a net investment in a foreign operation, which is a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign exchange risk in consolidated accounts.
